feed.title: SAP Community - Cloud Integration
feed.subtitle: Cloud Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Create Onboardee using API in ONB 2.0
entry.content:
I am working on building a CPI interface which will take some values like FN, LN, Position ID, Email.....,,, but, I do not have Person ID or Requesition ID (application id). 

I followed few SAP help notes and previous posts, unfortunately not able to derive proper methodology. I understand at the high level that I will have to create Person ID, which I did then I need to use API to createOnboardee which I think is a HTTP (link to a receiver) but how do i define it's body, also steps followed by this stage.

Thanks,

Ali

entry.title: Replication in SAP S4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am trying to retrigger the replication for some interface [initializations ] in order to resend all the records using the “Replicate by Replication Model” option.

Since the number of records is very large, I selected the Background Mode checkbox to avoid a dump. However, nothing appears in the message dashboard after triggering it.

PS: I have already tried triggering it several times, and the communication arrangements are properly set up and active.

Could you please help me resolve this issue?

Kind regards

Safa

entry.title: SAP CI integration with Cloud ALM monitoring
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

we will be using SAP Cloud ALM in the future (as successor for Solution Manager), among other tasks also for system and interface monitoring. Could Please guide about approach to integrate Integration Suite with Cloud ALM in order to monitor Integration Suite using Cloud ALM,including the main technical steps needed. How would you plan the implementation, which stakeholders would you involve and which challenges do you see? Please also give an outlook for additional ideas which you see as worthwhile for improving Integration Suite monitoring and operations.

Regards, 

Ramu.

entry.title: Getting 403 error while trying to download the deployed iflow using REST API
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am trying to download the deployed artifact using below url through browser and i can able to download a zip file.

Trying the same from postman with oauth giving me 403 - Forbidden error. I understand it lacks a permission as this is the same oauth used for accessing MessageProcessingLogs which is working. 

can someone help how do i achieve this so that i can replicate the same in python for internal work.

https://xxx.hana.ondemand.com/api/v1/IntegrationPackages('Testcicd')/$value

OAuth with NodeManager.read and IntegrationOperationServer.read permission

entry.title: Trading Partner Management: Indentification problems
entry.content:
Hello Colleagues,

I'm exploring the first time with SAP Integration Advisor - TPM configuration and I'm struggling to make the aggremment generate a correct trading partner ID reflect the reallity, after active the agreement, look the configuration below:

I'm testing the SOAP cenario Inbound in SAP CPI and I created the soap envelop with flat-file of ODETTE EDIFACT A96 S3 ORDERS:

Manually created SOAPEnvelop with FLAT FILE CDATA:

<soapenv:Envelope xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" xmlns:n0="http://sap.com/xi/SAPGlobal20/Global">
    <soapenv:Header/>
    <soapenv:Body>
        <n0:PurchaseOrderERPRequest_In>
            <MessageHeader>
                <ID>MSG_ODETTE_2026_999</ID>
                <CreationDateTime>2026-01-14T12:00:00Z</CreationDateTime>
                <SenderParty>
                    <InternalID schemeID="N/A" schemeAgencyID="ZZ" schemeAgencyName="ODETTE_EDIFACT">EXT.EDI</InternalID>
                </SenderParty>
                <RecipientParty>
                    <InternalID schemeID="N/A" schemeAgencyID="" schemeAgencyName="SAP">SAP</InternalID>
                </RecipientParty>
                <BusinessScope>
                    <TypeCode>PROCESS_ID</TypeCode>
                    <InstanceID>ODETTE_D96A_S3</InstanceID>
                </BusinessScope>
            </MessageHeader>
            <PurchaseOrder>
                <Payload>
                    <![CDATA[UNA:+.? '
UNB+UNOA:2+EXT.EDI:ZZ+SAP+260114:1830+REF12345'
UNH+1+ORDERS:D:96A:UN:S3'
BGM+220+PO999888777+9'
DTM+137:20260114:102'
PAI+:::42'
FTX+ZZZ+1++INSTRUCTIONS'
RFF+ON:4500001234'
NAD+BY+ID007::92'
NAD+SU+I008::92'
CUX+1:EUR:9'
LIN+1++PART-12345:IN'
QTY+21:1000:PCE'
DTM+2:202602011000:203'
UNS+S'
UNT+15+1'
UNZ+1+REF12345']]>
                </Payload>
            </PurchaseOrder>
        </n0:PurchaseOrderERPRequest_In>
    </soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>

Header:  UNB+UNOA:2+EXT.EDI:ZZ+SAP+260114:1830+REF12345'

Company Dummy:

I already setup the system and communication channel.

The indentifiers from the company

Indentifiers from the partner:

Created the agreement and active the agreement:

Agrement with MIG Sender, MAG and MIG Receiver:

The receiver channel in SAP basicalling I'm sending the result of the mapping in another endpoint in SAP CPI.

Agreement unic ID from trading partner:

SOAP-ODETTE_EDIFACT--D.96A S3-EXT.EDI-ZZ----EXT.EDI-ZZ----ORDERS - Wrong, it's always failed in the 2 Iflow Stantard 

Error:

javax.script.ScriptException: java.lang.Exception: java.lang.IllegalStateException: Partner ID SAP_TPM_467257935cff5475cbc45359b4ba5bc7 is not available.

It's like opositive way but I can't find the reason.

Partner Directory Lookup Keys:

SOAP-ODETTE_EDIFACT--D.96A S3-EXT.EDI-ZZ----SAP-----ORDERS

Agremment Plan ID:

SOAP-ODETTE_EDIFACT--D.96A S3-EXT.EDI-ZZ----EXT.EDI-ZZ----ORDERS

Someone could provide me support ?

Regards,

Viana

entry.title: SAP CPI: Can we use a JAR which has classes expecting input parameters as file path
entry.content:
Hi,

In CPI, we have a requirement to add a JAR file.

Classes inside jar are making API calls , fetching some key pairs and storing those in a local directory when used on local system. 

Can this jar be used somehow in cloud integration somehow?

Thanks & Regards,

Mayank

entry.title: SuccessFactors Custom Portlet Notifications via CPI to Microsoft Teams
entry.content:
Detailed Problem Description / Question

I’m looking for guidance (or an SAP KBA reference) around sending notifications from SAP SuccessFactors to Microsoft Teams via SAP CPI, specifically when the source is a custom portlet.

In our scenario, we have a custom portlet in SuccessFactors that displays an alert or status message on the SuccessFactors home page. Unlike standard SuccessFactors Teams notifications, this custom portlet does not automatically trigger a Teams notification.

The requirement is as follows:

  • A notification or alert occurs in a custom SuccessFactors portlet

  • This event should be picked up by SAP CPI

  • CPI should then send a notification to a Microsoft Teams channel (webhook)

At this stage, we understand that:

  • SuccessFactors does not natively generate MS Teams notifications for custom portlets

  • Any notification would likely require custom integration logic (e.g. OData, Integration Center extract, or custom API trigger)

  • CPI would be responsible for transforming the message and forwarding it to Microsoft Teams

My questions are:

  1. Is there an official SAP KBA or documentation that confirms custom portlets do not support native notifications?

  2. Are there recommended or supported approaches for enabling CPI to detect an event or change from a custom portlet?

  3. Has anyone successfully implemented a CPI → Microsoft Teams notification for a custom SuccessFactors object or portlet, and if so, what approach was used?

Best Regards

Adele Beets

entry.title: Looking for Expert and Community Insights on My SAP Technical Journey
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I hope you are all doing well.

I would like to kindly ask for your feedback and professional insights regarding my current learning journey within the SAP Technical domain. My goal is to continuously improve my technical profile and ensure that my learning path aligns well with real-world SAP projects and current market expectations.

Below is a brief overview of my learning direction and the reasoning behind each focus area.


SAP Technical Learning Direction At this stage, my primary focus is on pursuing the SAP Technical track. I am currently concentrating on SAP Basis, aiming to build a strong and solid foundation in SAP system administration, core architecture, and operational concepts.
In parallel, I am expanding my knowledge across several complementary technical areas, as outlined below:


SAP HANA
My objective is to gain a clear and comprehensive understanding of SAP HANA database architecture and how SAP systems interact with the database layer in terms of structure, performance optimization, and system integration.


SAP ABAP
I am working on building a solid understanding of the ABAP stack, with a focus on how development processes are designed and implemented within SAP systems. This helps me better understand system logic, debugging, and collaboration with development teams from a technical perspective.


SAP CPI (Cloud Integration)
My focus includes understanding SAP’s transition from traditional on-premise environments to cloud-based platforms, along with cloud integration concepts such as internal (A2A) and external (B2B) integration scenarios.

Additionally, I have complemented this learning path with the Apache Camel Framework, due to its importance and relevance within SAP integration solutions.


SAP UI5 / Fiori & SAP BTP CAPM
Recently, SAP introduced important updates related to both frontend and backend development models. I have been exploring these updates and following commonly recommended learning resources to better understand the modern SAP development approach, including CAP-based application development.


Learning Approach
Overall, I am intentionally following a structured and methodical learning sequence to build a holistic understanding of SAP Architecture as an integrated enterprise platform, rather than focusing on individual tools or isolated technologies.


Current Learning Path & Courses
Below are the main courses I am currently following (via Udemy):

SAP NetWeaver Fundamentals

SAP Basis Administration

SAP HANA Administration

Apache Camel Framework with Spring Boot

SAP Cloud Integration (CI / CPI), including API Management and Event Mesh

SAP UI5 / Fiori Full Stack with TypeScript / JavaScript using SAP BTP RAPM / CAPM


I would truly appreciate your feedback on whether this learning path is well-structured and aligned with real-world SAP implementations, and whether there are any key areas you would recommend adding, adjusting, or prioritizing differently.

Thank you in advance for your time and valuable insights.

Best regards,

entry.title: Is there any purchasing units import API in SAP Ariba
entry.content: I want to import purchasing units data from third-party system to Ariba system. I know that we can use import purchasing units task to import csv file to Ariba through ITK, But i think ITK have some disadvantage.  I want to implement this business scenario through an API call. Do you have some best practices ?

entry.title: SAP CPI - RFC Receiver interface does not works with BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT
entry.content:
Hello All,

I am working on an RFC receiver interface in SAP Cloud Integration where I am calling BAPI in SAP to create Orders. And I need to use the BAPIs "BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT" to commit the transaction for creation of Orders in SAP or  "BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK" to rollback in case of errors.
But even though I receive successful response during the BAPI call for Order creation, it seems like the COMMIT does not works. I searched on internet and I understood that this is most likely because of the two BAPI(creation and commit) calls are being made in different sessions. I need a little help to understand the possible options with RFC receiver adapter to handle this situation.
To add more about the scenario, the input to SAP CI might result in more than one different Orders and I need to COMMIT only after all these Orders are successful.
I have observed that the "Send Confirm Transaction" option in RFC adapter works, but it is committing individual Order creation, whereas I need to commit only after all Orders are successfully created otherwise "BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK" should be triggered.
Any help will be appreciated. Thanks!

Regards,
Faisal

entry.title: Need a help in CPI Mapping
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Need a help in achieving the below as values are coming as expected a step before the final field(Target) currently being Suppressed.

if E1EDK14/QUALF is 015 and E1EDK14/ORGID for values (ZS1, ZS10, ZS2, ZS6, ZS7, ZS8) then Prefix Minus Symbol (-) before E1EDS01/SUMME value
for E1EDS01/SUMID equals 010, 011 and 005 or else E1EDS01/SUMME.

entry.title: Disabling a pallete
entry.content:
Is there a way to actually disable a pallet while using SAP Cloud Integration ? I am new to SAP and have tried to search for this but haven't found anything thar leads me in this direction. 

Above is a very simple example of my use case. I want that at a time I could either use the groovy script or the HTTP call.
I know I could use Router in productive scenarios. This is just for testing purpose.

entry.title: Interface is in ABANDONED status
entry.content:
Dear All,

There is a Interface in NEO tenant and the Same interface runs everyday day - every hour. We have noticed that the Interface is in ABANDONED status 3 to 4 times in a day due to:

 

Error Details
Message processing was abandoned because of inactivity timeout of 24 hours
 
Please help us to identify the issue and its resolution.
Kindly let us know for any additional information and clarification?
 
Thanks in advance,
Yashodeep

entry.title: Pricing Integration using BAdI
entry.content:
Hi Team,

A pricing engine company would like to integrate their system to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and they would like to have their system integration to adhere to clean core principles of SAP for certification.

Originally it was setup for ECC which later converted to S/4HANA On Prem by using PRCD_ELEMENTS table manipulation to update the pricing in Sales Order but realize that this is not clean core compliant.

Due to time limitation, they are not able to create a new integration from scratch using SAP Cloud API and planning to use BAdI SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM to update the pricing conditions in sales orders. 

So the question would be, is this possible? And if it is, will this be clean core compliant?

Thank you.

entry.title: Third party integration document posting limitation
entry.content:
Dear All, 

We are in public cloud environment, using a point of sale system thirdparty, middleware of custom build, 

Purpose of middleware, all my point of sale system invoices will flow to middleware, from middleware we developed API and posting all the sale entiers, Customer, Sale order,  Batch, Serial, PGI, Delivery, Invoice happened through scheduling, Payment thorugh API. 

At present in a day we can only post 1300 documents, more than that for example, currently on week end we got 3000 invoices which it take 2 days to complete the posting. we need the best solution to hanlde it , if we want to manage up to 7000 documents.

entry.title: I am not able to open any of my iflows created in Integration suite and it gives me an error
entry.content:
hello experts,

I am not able to open any of my iflows and it gives me an error " The Content given is not a valid Integration Flow Bundle content" . I can see there is a note - 2691717 which mentions there might not be enough space in the tenant node and that one case has to be raised in component BC-CP-IS-OPS. Can someone guide me how to do this?

entry.title: Italy SDI B2G invoice send failing – cert status error 0010 (S4/CPI)
entry.content:
Hi team,

We have an issue. When we send invoices to Italy SDI (Italy Send Invoice) for B2G through S4/CPI, we receive the following error message "Invalid Certificate Status" error code 0010. However, all certificates are valid according to CPI Monitoring. Receiving invoices is still working.

Can anyone please help with this? How can we fix this issue?

entry.title: EDIT button missing in Entitlements at BTP
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Good day,

 

I'm trying to add "SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition" to our BTP Entitlements in Subaccount, however I cannot see the edit button from the entitlements please see image.

I believe I have the necessary roles for performing the steps from "SAP Discovery Center Mission Public Card - Setup SAP Build Work Zone", but I still can’t do it even though my authorization is already sufficient (Super Admin).

Please let me know your thoughts to resolve this.

 

Thank you in advance,

Best regards,

-Jaspher

entry.title: Azure databricks Integration using Cloud Integration(SAP IS)
entry.content:
hi Experts,

We are trying to connect Azure databricks Integration using Cloud Integration(SAP IS).. but the database types supported by Cloud Integration capability are the following ... Could you please suggest regarding the alternate options available to establish connectivity from Cloud Integration to Azure databricks.... Thank you!

-- Ramu.

entry.title: Beta Release of SAP Signavio Transformation Manager Objectives API
entry.content:
After the successful Beta launch of the Initiatives and Insights APIs , we are happy to announce the next addition to the SAP Signavio Transformation Manager API family: the Objectives API. 

With this launch, Objectives, the strategic foundation of your transformation initiatives, are now programmatically accessible. The Objectives API allows you to integrate your strategic objectives directly into your enterprise landscape and link them to initiatives, insights, and performance data. 

This is another important step towards SAP Signavio Transformation Manager becoming not only a cockpit for execution but also a fully integrated system for strategy-to-value orchestration. 

Why is the Objectives API important? 

In a typical transformation landscape, strategy and execution are decoupled: 

  • Objectives are maintained in strategy decks or portfolio management solutions. 
  • Initiatives are managed in project management systems.
  • Operational data is stored in analytics platforms. 
  • Governance is managed in other systems. 

This leads to a lack of integration. Alignment is done by hand. Reporting is inconsistent. 

The Objectives API fills this gap by allowing you to: 

  1. Strategically Integrate Across Systems 

You can now fetch and synchronize objectives from SAP Signavio directly into your enterprise applications using the REST API. 

This enables you to: 

  • Integrate transformation objectives into enterprise reporting. 
  • Synchronize portfolio dashboards with strategic objectives. 
  • Use consistent objective naming and metadata across systems. 
  1. Provide Clear Strategy-to-Execution Traceability 
  • The API allows you to fetch: 
  • A particular objective by its Suite Objective ID. 
  • The initiatives associated with a given objective. 

This establishes a direct, machine-readable connection between: 

  • Strategic goals (Objectives) 
  • Execution vehicles (Initiatives) 
  • Operational improvement signals (Insights) 

For transformation leaders, this means traceable value from strategy definition to value realization. 

  1. Standardized, Secure Access 
  • The Objectives API is consistent with the security framework of other Transformation Manager APIs: 
  • Standardized response formats 
  • Bearer authentication 

What’s Included in the Beta 

The Objectives API adds the following endpoint under: 

/transformationmanager/v1/objectives 

List Objectives 

Fetch, search and filter objectives with pagination support. 

Important parameters: 

  • page (zero-based index) 
  • size (max 1000) 
  • $count=true (returns total count) 

Response includes: 

  • Objective ID 
  • Name and description 
  • Creation and update timestamps 
  • Additional attributes (raw JSON): 
    • dateCreated 
    • dateUpdated 
    • name 
    • description 
    • attributes 
    • Etc. 

Get Objective by SID 

Fetch a specific objective by its Suite Objective ID: 

GET /objectives/by-sid/{objectiveSid} 

Get Initiatives for an Objective 

Fetch initiatives associated with a given objective: 

GET /objectives/by-sid/{objectiveSid}/initiatives 

This endpoint enables strategy-to-execution traceability by returning initiative summaries including: 

  • Status 
  • Value drivers 
  • Ownership and authorization data 
  • Timeline data 

Check this blog to learn more about Initiatives API.  

Example Integration Scenarios 

Enterprise Reporting Integration 

Synchronize objectives into: 

  • SAP Analytics Cloud dashboards 
  • Power BI reporting layers 
  • Executive portfolio dashboards 

This aligns strategic KPIs and transformation objectives on the same reporting model. 

Integrate Transformation Manager Objectives with OKR platforms (e.g., Tangible Growth): 

  • Synchronize strategic objectives between Tangible Growth and SAP Signavio Transformation Manager 
  • Map OKRs to linked objectives for execution traceability 
  • Reflect initiative status and progress back into OKR dashboards 
  • Maintain a consistent strategic reference model across systems 

This connects strategy definition (OKRs) with execution tracking (initiatives and insights), enabling closed-loop governance between planning and transformation delivery. 

Integrate Transformation Manager Objectives with OKR platforms (e.g., Tangible Growth): 

  • Synchronize strategic objectives between Tangible Growth and SAP Signavio Transformation Manager 
  • Map OKRs to linked objectives for execution traceability 
  • Reflect initiative status and progress back into OKR dashboards 
  • Maintain a consistent strategic reference model across systems 

This connects strategy definition (OKRs) with execution tracking (initiatives and insights), enabling closed-loop governance between planning and transformation delivery. 

Automation & Governance 

Automatically: 

  • Check if initiatives are connected to strategic objectives. 
  • Identify orphan initiatives. 
  • Launch workflows based on objective updates. 

AI and Advisory Scenarios 

Together with: 

  • Initiatives API 
  • Insights API 
  • AI Transformation Advisor functionality 

You can programmatically assess: 

  • Which objectives are under-supported. 
  • Which value drivers are over-represented. 
  • Where execution gaps are found. 

This enables data-driven portfolio management at scale. 

Who Should Explore the Objectives API 

  • Enterprise architects integrating strategy and execution tiers. 
  • Transformation leaders implementing portfolio governance. 
  • Developers implementing automation or AI use cases on SAP Signavio. 
  • Customers integrating Transformation Manager into their enterprise reporting infrastructure. 

If your company is already using the Initiatives and Insights APIs, the Objectives API rounds out the strategy-to-execution workflow. 

How to Get Started 

  1. Find the API documentation on SAP Business Accelerator Hub. 
  2. Explore the Objectives API endpoints and authentication configuration on SAP Help. 
  3. Test integration use cases in your sandbox environment. 
  4. Provide feedback to the SAP Signavio product team. 

Your feedback during the Beta phase will inform the General Availability roadmap. 

What’s Next 

After the Beta phase, we intend to: 

  • Enhance the Objectives API functionality. 
  • Intensify integration with value case modeling. 
  • Support more complex cross-asset querying. 
  • Reinforce strategy alignment patterns throughout the Suite.
